county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1858; antebellum county; North Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Suwannee County Clerk (Live Oak). Formed 1858 from Columbia. Named for the Suwannee River (Stephen Foster's 'Old Folks at Home'). Pre-1858 records in Columbia. Suwannee County Historical Museum (Live Oak) has local materials. Live Oak was a railroad junction town. Tobacco and timber industry records. Check Columbia and Alachua for earlier family records. Suwannee River area settlement patterns.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books for Suwannee County from 1858 forward; microfilm copies at Florida State Archives

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records for Suwannee County
source_profiles.survival_notes: County marriage records from formation; statewide recording began 1927

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1856; antebellum county; North Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Taylor County Clerk (Perry). Formed 1856 from Madison. Pre-1856 records in Madison. Gulf coast county known for timber and turpentine industry. Perry was a major lumber town. Taylor County Historical Society has local materials. Steinhatchee area fishing heritage. Forest Capital Museum State Park. Check Madison for earlier records. Fenholloway River area settlement. Very sparse population in the 19th century.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1921; 20th-century formation; North Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Union County Clerk (Lake Butler). Formed 1921 from Bradford — one of Florida's youngest counties and smallest by area. Pre-1921 records in Bradford (and earlier in Columbia as New River County). Lake Butler area. Union Correctional Institution (since 1913) records at Florida State Archives. Very limited local historical resources. Farming and turpentine industry heritage.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1854; antebellum county; Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Volusia County Clerk (DeLand). Formed 1854 from Orange (then Mosquito County). Daytona Beach area. Halifax Historical Society (Daytona Beach) and West Volusia Historical Society (DeLand) have local materials. Stetson University archives (DeLand). Pre-1854 records in Orange. Ponce Inlet Lighthouse area. Significant African American community — Bethune-Cookman University (Mary McLeod Bethune) archives in Daytona Beach. Early tourist and racing history records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1843; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); Panhandle region
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Wakulla County Clerk (Crawfordville). Formed 1843 from Leon during the territorial period. Pre-1843 records in Leon. Small Gulf coast county south of Tallahassee. San Marcos de Apalache Historic State Park (Spanish fort). Wakulla Springs area. Wakulla County Historical Society has limited materials. St. Marks area was an important colonial port. Check Leon County and Florida State Archives for earlier records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1824; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); Panhandle region
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Walton County Clerk (DeFuniak Springs). Formed 1824 from Escambia and Jackson. BURNED COUNTY — 1885 courthouse fire destroyed early records. DeFuniak Springs was a Chautauqua assembly site (1885+). Walton County Heritage Museum has local collections. Pre-1824 records in Escambia and Jackson. Check Florida State Archives for surviving state copies and tax rolls. Eden Gardens State Park area. Panhandle pioneer families.

county_metadata.record_losses: 1890 courthouse fire destroyed many early records
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1825; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); Panhandle region
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Washington County Clerk (Chipley). Formed 1825 from Jackson and Walton. BURNED COUNTY — 1890 courthouse fire destroyed many early records. Pre-1825 records in Jackson and Walton. Panhandle county with strong ties to Alabama. Chipley was a railroad town named for railroad executive William Chipley. Washington County Historical Society has local materials. Falling Waters State Park. Check Florida State Archives for surviving state copies. Many families migrated from Alabama and Georgia.
